We recommended that Management require all Accountable Officers to settle their respective cash advances at the end of the year to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the books of accounts of the Agency.  Henceforth, avoid granting subsequent cash advances to Accountable Officers with outstanding cash advances and withhold the salaries and/or any amount due to those Accountable Officers who were unable to comply with the pertinent rules and regulations on cash advances. Moreover, adhere strictly to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and settlement of cash advances as enunciated in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
We further recommended the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances amounting to ₱160,394.28 as of December 31, 2021 and submit the liquidation report or proof of refund/s, if there is any, to the Auditor for post-audit. observations.recommendation:
We commended Management for optimally utilizing its 20% Economic Development Fund, thus, attaining its commitment to its constituents to deliver the benefits that can be derived from the projects that were implemented.

Further, we recommended that Management continue to pursue improved practices in programming, prioritizing and implementing programs and projects to maximize the utilization of the Local Development Fund in attaining desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community.

Moreover, we recommended that Management expedite the implementation and completion of the lone unimplemented and four on-going projects, during the ensuing CY 2021.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the DRRM funds received from National Government from the GF bank account to the TF bank account. Further, the OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the correcting entry recording DRRM funds received from the National Government under the Trust Liabilities-DRRM account in the Trust Fund books instead of the Due to Other NGAs account in the General Fund books.

Also, we recommended that in the succeeding Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, the OIC-Municipal Accountant include as part of the sources of DRRMF the balance of the cash donations totaling ₱0.51 million in CYs 2017, 2019 and 2020 and the fund transfers from the National Government amounting ₱9.59 million in CY 2020 which remained unutilized as of December 31, 2020.

Henceforth, all cash donations received, whether from local or foreign donors, and fund transfers from the National Government and other LGUs shall be presented as part of the sources of DRRMF and its disbursement included in the utilization portion of the report.

Further, considering that the cash donations amounting ₱0.51 million are already considered funds of the Municipality but may only be used for the purpose for which these were intended which, unfortunately, is already past due, Management should evaluate if there are expenses or activities related to the purpose of the donations that can still be charged to said funds or seek authority from the concerned donors to allow the Municipality to reprogram the cash donations for other DRRM projects or programs in order that it may be properly utilized.

We commended the Municipal Government for complying with the posting and reporting requirements as required in Item 4.2, 4.3 and 4.4 of DBM LBC No. 125.  

Further, we recommended that Management strictly comply with the posting requirement of the procurements undertaken through Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act on the GPPB Online Portal to enhance transparency and accountability.

We recommended that Management adopt the recommended procedural flowchart in the procurement and distribution of food supplies (Annex 10) pursuant to the afore-cited law and regulations.  The following are the recommended changes in the procedures:

The inspection of deliveries of food supplies purchased shall be conducted by the GSO and shall be accepted in the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR). 

 A Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) (numbered based on RIS logbook) shall be prepared in three (3) copies to turn over the responsibility of the goods from the GSO to the MSWDO: 

	Copy 1- Accounting
      Copy 2- Requesting Party                   
      Copy 3- File Copy of GSO

The custody of the said food supplies shall be the responsibility of the MSWDO.

The Municipal Accountant shall adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording the food supplies procured. The AIR and other supporting documents shall be the basis in the preparation of the DV and recording of deliveries in the books and the appropriate ledger cards.

The MSWDO shall be responsible for the proper distribution of the food supplies to the barangays/recipients. A sub-RIS in four (4) copies shall be prepared for the request for issuance of food supplies, distributed as follows:
                   
                   Copy 1- Accounting
                   Copy 2- Requesting Party                   
                   Copy 3- File Copy of GSO
                   Copy 4- File copy of MSWDO 
                
The MSWDO shall number the sub-RIS based on a sub-RIS Logbook maintained for the purpose.  The sub-RIS Logbook shall have the following column headings: sub-RIS No., Date, Description and Quantity of Food Supplies to be Issued, Name of Requesting Party, Position/Designation, Official Station or Address and Signature.

After properly accomplishing and signing the sub-RIS, the MSWDO shall issue the food supplies to the barangay officials authorized to the receive the supplies.

The MSWDO shall submit to the GSO, on a weekly or on a monthly basis, the 1st and 3rd copy of the sub-RIS.  The GSO shall promptly prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) based on the RIS and sub-RIS which shall be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for recording.

The Municipal Accountant shall prepare a JEV to record the food supplies expense based on the SSMI received from the GSO.

The MSWDO shall require all requesting parties in the sub-RIS to submit the properly accomplished RDL within 15 days after the distribution of the food supplies to the beneficiaries.  They shall conduct regular monitoring of the submission thereof and follow up with the concerned requesting party who failed to submit the RDL.

The MSWDO shall prepare and submit the liquidation report per RIS, once the RDLs are complete, to the Municipal Accountant for submission to the Auditor and to monitor the liquidation thereof, instead of directly attaching the documents to the disbursement vouchers.  

The Municipal Accountant and the GSO shall regularly reconcile their respective records to determine any errors in recording of food supplies procurement and issuances.

The PPMPs shall be submitted by each Department/Office of the Municipal Government to the Municipal Budget Office and shall be evaluated and included in the proposed Annual Budget for approval. After approval of the proposed Annual Budget, the Municipal Budget Office shall furnish a copy of the same, together with the corresponding PPMPs to the BAC Secretariat for its review and consolidation into the proposed APP. 

The BAC shall evaluate the APP and determine the applicable mode of procurement for each goods or services to be procured contained therein.  After the evaluation, the BAC shall issue a Resolution recommending the modes of procurement for all the items contained in the APP and forwards the same, with the APP to the Mayor for approval.  

A copy of the BAC Resolution, together with the approved APP shall be furnished the GSO as basis for the preparation of Purchase Request on goods or services needed for the quarter at the beginning of the year and of each subsequent quarter.

Further, we recommended that the Management submit a summary list of the distributed relief items for CY 2020, with details of the quantities and details of the relief items and the balances of the relief items as of December 31, 2020, if there is any.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation and submission of the BRS for December 2020, together with the corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs), paid/cancelled checks and original copies of Bank Statements and Debit and Credit Memos for review and verification, and
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant submit the BRS within ten days after the end of the month. Make representation with the depository bank to ensure prompt delivery of the Bank Statements. Otherwise, secure copies of snapshots of the LGU’s transactions to facilitate the preparation of the BRS.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant, based on the Bank Reconciliation Statement shall revert the total amount of stale checks outstanding in the hands of the payees to the Cash-in Bank and Accounts Payable accounts to ensure the proper presentation of the account balances; and
The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit all the cancelled checks on hand for validation and, henceforth, ensure that all cancelled checks are attached to the Report of Checks Issued in accordance with the prescribed procedures of Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, at the beginning of the year, submit a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, which will be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in recording the Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable and the Deferred Real Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that Management form a Reconciliation Team composed of least one capable member coming from the Municipal Accounting Office, Property Unit/Staff of the OIC-GSO and Municipal Engineering Office (for the infrastructure assets, buildings and other structures) who shall be temporarily relieved of their regular duties until such time that the reconciliation process is completed.

We recommended that the Management hire a permanent staff who shall assist the designated GSO-designate to maintain property subsidiary records such as Property Cards and Local Road Network Property Cards (LRNPCs) of all existing constructed and acquired properties of the Municipality based on available records and documents.
Further, the GSO-Designate and the Municipal Assessor shall maintain duplicate copies of the OCTs/TCTs and other relevant documents of the real properties owned by the Municipal Government. The Municipal Treasurer shall have custody of the titles to the real properties of the LGU.
Moreover, the Inventory Committee to determine and indicate in the RPCPPE the physical condition of the infrastructure assets, buildings and other structures for proper measurement of its carrying amount in the financial statements and to update the current use of lots/land for proper classification in the books by the OIC- Municipal Accountant; and
Finally, Management to consider organizing and establishing the General Services Office to be headed by a permanent Department Head to take on the duties and responsibilities as provided under Section 490 of the Local Government Code due to the increasing number of assets owned by the LGU that needs to be accounted for.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
Inventory Committee to attach the Inventory Tag/Stickers to the movable properties immediately after the physical inventory;
OIC-GSO to renew and update all the AREs and check whether all movable properties with updated AREs were included in the RPCPPE and to require the accountable officer/s to present the PPE item/s, if any, which were not included in the physical count by the Inventory Committee; and
Inventory Committee to indicate and properly identify the accountable officer for each item of property in the RPCPPE.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to determine the details of the PPEs with lump-sum balances for CY 2015 and prior years recorded in their computerized accounting system using the PPELC and other source documents in order that the balances appearing in the SLs may be ascertained.

We recommended that Management instruct the designated General Service Officer and OIC - Municipal Accountant to maintain complete Property Cards for each category of PPE and Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLC), respectively, for all real/immovable properties owned by the Municipality following the prescribed forms in the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We commended Management for optimally utilizing its 20% Economic Development Fund, thus, attaining its commitment to its constituents to deliver the benefits that can be derived from the projects that were implemented.
Further, we recommended that Management continue to pursue improved practices in programming, prioritizing and implementing programs and projects to maximize the utilization of the Local Development Fund in attaining desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community.
Moreover, we recommended that Management expedite the implementation and completion of the lone unimplemented and four on-going projects, during the ensuing CY 2021.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the DRRM funds received from National Government from the GF bank account to the TF bank account. Further, the OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the correcting entry recording DRRM funds received from the National Government under the Trust Liabilities-DRRM account in the Trust Fund books instead of the Due to Other NGAs account in the General Fund books.
Also, we recommended that in the succeeding Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, the OIC-Municipal Accountant include as part of the sources of DRRMF the balance of the cash donations totaling ₱0.51 million in CYs 2017, 2019 and 2020 and the fund transfers from the National Government amounting ₱9.59 million in CY 2020 which remained unutilized as of December 31, 2020.
Henceforth, all cash donations received, whether from local or foreign donors, and fund transfers from the National Government and other LGUs shall be presented as part of the sources of DRRMF and its disbursement included in the utilization portion of the report.
Further, considering that the cash donations amounting ₱0.51 million are already considered funds of the Municipality but may only be used for the purpose for which these were intended which, unfortunately, is already past due, Management should evaluate if there are expenses or activities related to the purpose of the donations that can still be charged to said funds or seek authority from the concerned donors to allow the Municipality to reprogram the cash donations for other DRRM projects or programs in order that it may be properly utilized.

We commended the Municipal Government for complying with the posting and reporting requirements as required in Item 4.2, 4.3 and 4.4 of DBM LBC No. 125.  Further, we recommended that, henceforth, Management strictly comply with the posting requirement of the procurements undertaken through Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act on the GPPB Online Portal to enhance transparency and accountability.

We recommended that Management adopt the recommended procedural flowchart in the procurement and distribution of food supplies (Annex 10) pursuant to the afore-cited law and regulations.  The following are the recommended changes in the procedures:
The inspection of deliveries of food supplies purchased shall be conducted by the GSO and shall be accepted in the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR).
A Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) (numbered based on RIS logbook) shall be prepared in three (3) copies to turn over the responsibility of the goods from the GSO to the MSWDO:
Copy 1- Accounting
Copy 2- Requesting Party
Copy 3- File Copy of GSO
The custody of the said food supplies shall be the responsibility of the  MSWDO.
The Municipal Accountant shall adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording the food supplies procured. The AIR and other supporting documents shall be the basis in the preparation of the DV and recording of deliveries in the books and the appropriate ledger cards.
The MSWDO shall be responsible for the proper distribution of the food supplies to the barangays/recipients. A sub-RIS in four (4) copies shall be prepared for the request for issuance of food supplies, distributed as follows:
Copy 1- Accounting
Copy 2- Requesting Party
Copy 3- File Copy of GSO
Copy 4- File copy of MSWDO
The MSWDO shall number the sub-RIS based on a sub-RIS Logbook maintained for the purpose.  The sub-RIS Logbook shall have the following column headings: sub-RIS No., Date, Description and Quantity of Food Supplies to be Issued, Name of Requesting Party, Position/Designation, Official Station or Address and Signature.
After properly accomplishing and signing the sub-RIS, the MSWDO shall issue the food supplies to the barangay officials authorized to the receive the supplies.
The MSWDO shall submit to the GSO, on a weekly or on a monthly basis, the 1st and 3rd copy of the sub-RIS.  The GSO shall promptly prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) based on the RIS and sub-RIS which shall be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for recording.
The Municipal Accountant shall prepare a JEV to record the food supplies expense based on the SSMI received from the GSO.
The MSWDO shall require all requesting parties in the sub-RIS to submit the properly accomplished RDL within 15 days after the distribution of the food supplies to the beneficiaries.  They shall conduct regular monitoring of the submission thereof and follow up with the concerned requesting party who failed to submit the RDL.
The MSWDO shall prepare and submit the liquidation report per RIS, once the RDLs are complete, to the Municipal Accountant for submission to the Auditor and to monitor the liquidation thereof, instead of directly attaching the documents to the disbursement vouchers.
The Municipal Accountant and the GSO shall regularly reconcile their respective records to determine any errors in recording of food supplies procurement and issuances.
The PPMPs shall be submitted by each Department/Office of the Municipal Government to the Municipal Budget Office and shall be evaluated and included in the proposed Annual Budget for approval. After approval of the proposed Annual Budget, the Municipal Budget Office shall furnish a copy of the same, together with the corresponding PPMPs to the BAC Secretariat for its review and consolidation into the proposed APP.
The BAC shall evaluate the APP and determine the applicable mode of procurement for each goods or services to be procured contained therein.  After the evaluation, the BAC shall issue a Resolution recommending the modes of procurement for all the items contained in the APP and forwards the same, with the APP to the Mayor for approval.
A copy of the BAC Resolution, together with the approved APP shall be furnished the GSO as basis for the preparation of Purchase Request on goods or services needed for the quarter at the beginning of the year and of each subsequent quarter.
Further, we recommended that the Management submit a summary list of the distributed relief items for CY 2020, with details of the quantities and details of the relief items and the balances of the relief items as of December 31, 2020, if there is any.

We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the RPLCs for Land.

We recommended that:
The Property Custodian keep a complete and updated LRNPCs to serve as a means of checking the accuracy of the Local Road Network account maintained by the Municipal Accountant.
Both the Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant conduct regular reconciliation of the balances of their records, at least on a quarterly basis, to ensure that their records are consistent with each other.
The Municipal Engineer be required to furnish the Municipal Accountant and the Designated GSO with complete description and cost segregation of the various road components for completed road projects as basis for recording in their respective subsidiary records.

We reiterate the prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a request for authority to write off the dormant Due from LGUs account amounting to ₱307,971 following the procedures in Sections 8.2 and 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue to trace and analyze the transactions, and reconcile the difference of ₱3.27 million in the reciprocal accounts Due to/Due from Other Funds and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the accounts.

We recommended that LDRRM Council shall plan and decide on the programs and projects that shall be funded by the LDRRMF and Management shall ensure that these are fully implemented during the year in order that the funds are efficiently and effectively utilized.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Make the necessary correcting entries to record the transfer the cost of Buildings amounting to ₱3.72 million from the TF books to the GF books and, thereafter, recognize the corresponding depreciation on the Buildings in the GF books;
Reconcile the difference in the beginning balance of the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account amounting to  ₱7.59 million per NTFS and SL by tracing the unrecorded disbursements charged against the DRRMF-STF in the previous years and, thereafter, make the necessary correcting entries; and
Reconcile the difference in the ending balance of Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account per SFP and SL amounting to 1.05 million.

We recommended that Management allocate at least one per cent of its annual DRRM Fund budget for MDM and, thereafter, integrate in their LDRRM Plan policies, standards, guidelines, systems and procedures for MDM operations and research and development.

We commended Management for the increase in their collections for CY 2019 compared with the previous year, however, we also recommended that it undertake the following actions to improve its collection efficiency:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer to carefully review and assess their revenue projections for each income account to ensure systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue collections for the ensuing year; and
Thereafter, require the Municipal Treasurer to conduct periodic review and take the necessary actions to ensure collection targets are attained and collection efficiency is improved.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Point to administer the HGDG tests in the identification and selection of PPAs during the budget and AR preparation to serve as basis in determining the actual expenditures that can be attributed in the GAD Budget.

We recommended that Management direct the setting up of a GAD Database that will contain statistics and sex-disaggregated data systematically gathered and regularly updated to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation and subjected to gender analysis, for proper identification of existing gender issues to ensure that the approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are able to address these issues.

We recommended that the LCE issue an executive order establishing the Monitoring and Evaluation Team to facilitate the evaluation of the GAD-related accomplishments and the preparation of the GAD Evaluation Report.
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to make a thorough analysis of the unremitted withholding taxes and remit to the BIR without further delay any amount due.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to make a thorough analysis of the unremitted contributions and loans repayment of officials and employees to GSIS, Philhealth and PAG-IBIG and to remit the same without further delay.
Further, in case penalties are imposed by the concerned agencies on the delayed remittance of the mandatory deductions, it shall be for the personal account of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the immediate reconciliation of the individual balances of the Cash in Local Treasury and Petty Cash Fund accounts between the GL and the Cashbooks for all funds and make the necessary adjustments thereon. Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant shall reconcile their books of accounts at least quarterly to avoid incurrence of accumulated unreconciled balances in compliance with existing regulation.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that Management take the following immediate courses of actions:

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to record in the books all the reconciling items, after a thorough analysis thereof, and collating the necessary supporting documents to substantiate the same, for a fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements;

Direct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to submit and record in the books the 61 unrecorded disbursement vouchers totaling ₱2,022,056.32 and thereafter to submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for audit purposes, otherwise Notices of suspension or disallowance shall be issued on said accounts; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to analyze thoroughly and record in the books the reconciling items after the preparation of the BRS to ensure accurate recording of accounts in the books of accounts, thereby allowing fair presentation of accounts in the year-end financial statements.
